intro:
There is not enough information available about Tithymalus aulacospermus. However, I can provide you with general information about the Tithymalus genus, which may be helpful.

The Tithymalus genus is a group of plants in the Euphorbiaceae family, commonly known as spurge. The plants within this genus are typically succulents, producing milky latex and bearing small, white or yellow flowers. 

While information on Tithymalus aulacospermus specifically is limited, the Tithymalus genus, in general, has been studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of the Tithymalus genus may include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids.

The most common herbal preparations made with plants of the Tithymalus genus, such as Tithymalus communis, are typically used to make tinctures, infusions, and ointments.
